TextENGNew: "...That doesn't make sense.@k@vS10/62/410101298. If you loved him... then why did you kill him?"
Perhaps that question was far too naive to pose to a human being.
changeReason: Changed 'imprudent' to 'naive'; shifts meaning from rudeness to simplistic understanding, better matching JPN 浅はか (shallow/naive)

TextENGNew: Love and hatred are two sides of the same coin. The deeper those feelings run, the more easily a spark can flip the positive to negative, affection to loathing.
And... sometimes that emotional shift becomes a murderous impulse, generating an urge to attack the other person.
changeReason: Changed 'violent urges' to 'murderous impulse'; new version matches JPN 殺したいほどの衝動 (impulse to kill) more precisely

TextENGNew: But I'd always believed that the people who actually crossed that line were rare.
No matter how fiercely you hate or reject someone... if they still matter to you more than anything, you should be able to accept it or compromise... no, you should be able to.
changeReason: New version adds self-correcting 'no, you should be able to' matching JPN いや、できるはずなんだ; old version used 'it's important' which misses the narrator's self-reassurance

TextENGNew: If someone couldn't tolerate even that and actually carried out their murderous intent... it would mean they'd abandoned any attachment to love and denied everything they'd once felt.
"I don't get it...@k@vS10/62/410101300. Did that boyfriend of yours betray you or something?@k@vS10/62/410101301. Is that why you killed him?"
changeReason: Changed 'Something unforgivable?' to 'Is that why you killed him?'; rephrases from asking about forgiveness to directly asking about murder motivation

TextENGNew: "No... Takumi-kun would never do that.@k@vS10/42/410600278. I just... wanted to erase him. That's all."
changeReason: Changed 'get rid of' to 'erase'; JPN 消す implies making someone cease to exist rather than removing an inconvenience

TextENGNew: "Erase...?"
changeReason: Changed 'get rid of' to 'erase'; JPN 消す implies making someone cease to exist rather than removing an inconvenience

TextENGNew: "It's been nearly seven years since Takumi-kun and I started dating.@k@vS10/42/410600280. So long, yet so short...@k@vS10/42/410600281. We first met during my first year of college..."
Towada-san raised her head and gazed off in the distance.
That moment, an image emerged on the back of her eyelids. A little old school building, not far from the city center...
A massive green lawn was visible from the fourth floor of the school building.
Our class was in the very last classroom on the far end of the building.
I finally got accepted into the college I had been hoping to attend, so everything was looking bright.

...But things were a bit difficult for me because I was a year younger than my classmates... I started to develop an inferiority complex about it... and after enrolling, I spent most of my time alone.
"Hey, Towada-san.@k@vS10/00/410700002. Are you interested in attending a live concert next Sunday?"
One day,
a male classmate spoke to me for the very first time.
He was still quite young and had a youthful look on his face.
He looked a little scrawny, and his clothes were a bit sloppy. ...But I was fascinated by the huge, pure smile on his face.
"A concert...?"
"Yeah, a concert.@k@vS10/00/410700004. Do you like music, Towada-san?"
"Y-yeah... a little.@k@vS10/42/410600285. Who is performing at this concert?"
"I am. Me.@k@vS10/00/410700006. Who else would it be?"
"I'm... putting together a band.@k@vS10/00/410700008. Actually, I write my own songs too... and I'm rather proud of my work."
...Honestly, I was totally shocked.

I didn't have any special skills of my own, so for someone to be able to pick up music while also studying for school, it felt like he was from a totally different world... Not just a celebrity, but a divine being.
He even did his own composition and spoke to people to convince them to attend his concerts. That's not something you can do without a great deal of confidence in your skills.
And a person with such unprecedented talent was standing directly in front of me, right now...
So, I was completely star-struck... It was a wonderful feeling.
"So? ...How about it?@k@vS10/00/410700010. If you're busy, I understand..."
I was so stunned by the sudden situation that I couldn't find the words. Perhaps due to that, his voice suddenly grew uneasy.
...After seeing that, I understood.
Of course. This guy was putting on a brave face for my sake.
All just for me, the one who stuck out in our class...
"...Is that really ok?@k@vS10/42/410600289. I don't know much about music though..."
"O-of course! That's fine!@k@vS10/00/410700012. It'll be great, I promise!"
As soon as I responded that it was ok, he put on a huge, bright smile.
Honestly, he was like a child...
But I found that innocence wonderful, from the bottom of my heart.
"So... where are you performing?@k@vS10/42/410600291. Can you tell me the location?"
"R-right! Here's a ticket and the flyer!@k@vS10/00/410700014. ...Oh, don't worry about money! It's completely free!@k@vS10/00/410700015. Alright, it's a promise then! I'll be waiting for you!!"
Takumi-kun wrapped his little finger around my little finger as he promised that with a beaming smile on his face, then he ran out of the classroom.
...That was my first time locking fingers with a boy.
My heart... was beating fast.
And it didn't take much time after that before we got together.
Since we were in the same class, we were always together. The two of us went to all sorts of places together every week, and I went to see all his concerts.
He... Takumi Kurosawa-kun was playing the guitar, and was fortunate enough to have a bunch of fans.
Fan girls would come to the concerts and stare longingly at Takumi-kun. There were so many girls for Takumi-kun to choose from, and they were always cheering for him.
But even so... Takumi-kun's eyes were always on me.
I was the one he cherished most.
And that made me so happy... It was a blessing.
"I'm seriously aiming to go pro after college."

"I've always wanted to make a living off my music.@k@vS10/00/410700018. ...As long as you're with me, Yae, and I can make a living just playing my music, I don't need anything else...@k@vS10/00/410700019. And so, I've decided."
...I enjoyed hearing Takumi-kun talk about his dreams.
Takumi-kun's eyes glittered and sparkled any time he told me about his future ambitions.
If we ever move in together, I want to get a dachshund.
I'd like to live near a big park, and try doing the radio exercises together every morning, even as we grow old.
I want to play a concert at the Budokan, and propose to you right after finishing my best song...
He was seriously considering these things, laughing like a child all the while.
But... despite that, I was feeling the opposite. I was quite anxious.
If he really does make a big hit and become a celebrity, he'll be surrounded by fans and other stars... If that were to happen, would Takumi-kun's feelings for me stay the same?
I believe that he loves me very much right now. ...But as his position changes, his point of view will change... and his values along with it.
When that happens... will I be able to follow him?
Or... will he leave me behind?
That dreadful future haunted my thoughts. Sometimes even my dreams...
And so, there came a day when I asked him about it.
"Hey, Takumi-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600295. If some horrible disaster plagued our world and forced you to choose between your music or me... which would you choose?"
"Huh? What is this...?@k@vS10/00/410700021. What is that supposed to mean?"
"Uh, umm... well.@k@vS10/42/410600297. I saw a story like that on TV the other day..."
That was a lie, of course. It truly was a ridiculous nonsensical question.
But Takumi-kun smiled like usual... and without hesitation, he gave me an answer with a serious look on his face.
"I dunno what sort of horrible disaster you're talking about, but...@k@vS10/00/410700023. If my only options are you or my music... I'd definitely choose you, Yae."
"...Really?"
"Of course.@k@vS10/00/410700025. I could live without my music, but honestly, I don't even want to imagine life without you."
"Takumi-kun..."
I was elated.
He wasn't just saying that. It felt like he truly meant it... I was deeply moved.
I always felt warm and happy when I was with Takumi-kun. Just being around him made me feel at ease...
"Takumi-kun... I love you."
"...You idiot.@k@vS10/00/410700027. I love you even more than you can imagine."
"No way! I..."
I started to speak up, but Takumi-kun kissed me.
Those were a sweet, delightful four years.
I graduated from college and got a job doing clerical work for a small trading company.
It was a company I had never heard of and it didn't have very many employees, but the company was doing solid business.
Takumi-kun, on the other hand, couldn't find a job anywhere.
He sincerely wanted to concentrate all his strength on his music career.
Takumi-kun's life hadn't changed much since his college days, just that the time he spent in lectures was replaced with a part-time job. The band members' faces didn't change either.
Just like in our college days, I attended Takumi-kun's concerts every week.
So, our relationship never really settled down.
Nevertheless, over the coming years... the people around us started getting married and skipping the band to take care of their kids.
Takumi-kun's band members started giving up on the band, one after another...
"Takumi-kun, are you all right...?@k@vS10/42/410600303. You've been feeling down recently, haven't you?"
"I'm all right.@k@vS10/00/410700029. ...I'm just busy at work, so I'm not getting enough sleep."
Although Takumi-kun said as much, I could tell he was feeling irritated.
He was having trouble replacing missing band members recently, and he was struggling to find the money to open a studio, or put on any concerts.
Our college friends were getting ready to buy condos or build houses.
But Takumi-kun was still struggling with the fact that his musical career wasn't opening up, even after all this time... He looked deeply troubled.
So one day, when I came back from work...
"Hey, Takumi-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600305. Why don't we live together?"
I was the one to suggest it.
I knew that Takumi-kun was suffering, so I wanted to be by his side to make it better, even if only a little bit... I wanted to do whatever I could to help.

"If we live together, our expenses will be cut in half...@k@vS10/42/410600307. And if we cook for ourselves properly, we can save a lot of money there too.@k@vS10/42/410600308. If we put some of that money toward your music career, maybe it'll make your troubles a little lighter."
"...Are you really ok with that?@k@vS10/00/410700032. Yae, you..."
"It's fine.@k@vS10/42/410600310. I want to do it."
Takumi-kun didn't answer right away. ...That seemed natural. Regardless of how I felt about it, I'd be formally agreeing to cover part of his expenses... no, perhaps almost all of them.
However... I had already decided. And I was fine with it.
"Takumi-kun, you're able to do things I could never do.@k@vS10/42/410600312. You have incredible talent, beyond my imagination.@k@vS10/42/410600313. So, I want to support you."
"There's a world out there I could never see on my own, but if I could see even a little of it standing by your side... would that be so bad?"
"No, but..."

"I'm sure it'll happen.@k@vS10/42/410600316. ...It's all right, I believe in you.@k@vS10/42/410600317. So let me make you believe too, Takumi-kun.@k@vS10/42/410600318. In the lovely world from your dreams you're having now..."
Then Takumi-kun hugged me so tight that it hurt... and I cried, just a little.
Despite the pain in my arms.
Despite the burning of those teardrops.
I felt happy... Yes, I truly did feel happy...
And then, we started living together.
There were all sorts of problems that came from the two of us living together, and it was more difficult than I thought... but that was all blown away by the tremendous joy I got out of it.
"I... don't want to be a bother for you, Yae...@k@vS10/00/410700037. If you're unhappy, you're allowed to tell me about it, you know?"
"Yeah..."
"Yae... you'll still be standing by my side when I make my major debut, right?"
"Well, of course...?@k@vS10/42/410600321. I'll support you.@k@vS10/42/410600322. I'll stand by your side... as you chase your dreams, Takumi-kun.@k@vS10/42/410600323. ...Because I love you."
Takumi-kun was always so kind.
On paydays, he would always bring home my favorite cake.
Just remembering those days brings a tear to my eye...
We were so full of love... Those days are irreplaceable...
However.
"...Shortly after that, Takumi-kun started to change."
Towada-san turned her gaze downward.
And the next moment...
*thump*
I heard a sound coming from the doorway.
Sonozaki-san's complexion quickly changed as she rose to her feet. She grabbed the sword resting by her side and stood there without making a sound.
"Sonozaki-san...?!"
"Quiet!"
Sonozaki-san held her breath and listened carefully.
*rustle*
Someone is there... just outside the assembly hall...!
That was the sound of another person moving around, I'm certain of it...!
Miyuki-chan's face turned pale as she suddenly reached into her knapsack.
Towada-san held her mouth shut, while Otobe and I focused all the strength in our bodies on listening to the sounds coming from outside.
"What the hell is this...?"
"W... who on earth could it be...?"
Sonozaki-san turned back toward Otobe who was trembling and shouting, and held a finger to her mouth.
"You guys need to stay here."
"What about you...?"
"I have to do a little cleaning up."
After that proclamation, Sonozaki-san unsheathed her sword and walked toward the entrance.
"W-wait... Sonozaki-san...?@k@|@y@vS10/62/410101306. Wha?!"
I started to chase after her, but something immediately startled me.
I heard the sound of someone fiddling with a gun.
...The thing Miyuki-chan pulled out of her knapsack was a black handgun.
"M-Miyuki-chan...?!"
I was so shocked my voice was quivering.
We were all listening to Towada-san's story until a moment ago, but the situation blew up in an instant.
"Sonozaki-san, I'll go too."
"...@k@vS10/43/410400421.Very well. Come on."
"W... what the hell are you guys...?!"
I was so shocked I couldn't string together a sentence.
Sonozaki-san's katana was alarming enough, but... Miyuki-chan... W-why did she have a gun...?!
"Ryuunosuke-san, please stay here with the others."
"Y-you know we can't do that!"
I rushed after Miyuki-chan.
"M... me too. I'll go with you...!"
Otobe also scrambled to his feet. ...But it seemed pretty clear that he was following after us out of fear.
I don't have the courage to enter a battlefield where people are armed with katanas and guns any more than he does.
But... I'm even more afraid of staying here...!
Just as I was about to leave the room, I looked back to see Towada-san still sitting idly on the floor, gazing into Kurosawa's face.
"Towada-san..."
"...I'll stay here."
"Staying here may cost you your life."
"...Understood."
Sonozaki-san stared at Towada-san for a moment, then let out a sigh before walking out.
How could she kill the man she loved...?
She spoke calmly as she recounted her story, but the pain and sorrow were still there.
But I still hadn't heard her reason for killing him.
Will we survive long enough to hear the rest...?
I lamented leaving Towada-san behind while I made my way out of the area.
The rain was still coming down.
I couldn't see anything outside. The surrounding area was completely swallowed by the darkness.
"Stay still until your eyes adjust.@k@vS10/43/410400424. ...And don't make a sound."
After she said that, I held my breath and took a look around.
...I can't see anything. Why is the air so tense?!
Miyuki-chan stood beside me with a gun in hand, focusing her ears on the darkness around us.
Suddenly...
I heard Otobe's footsteps.
"Wah?! S-sorry...!"
"It's fine, just be quiet!"
The next moment, I heard the voices of a few men and could see several flashlights waving all around us.
"They're here!"
"Where?!"
"Get down!"
We ducked down to avoid the flashlights. ...However, the voices were gradually growing closer and more numerous.
Sonozaki-san tightly clenched the handle of her sword.
"...No good.@k@vS10/43/410400428. ...Looks like I'll have to clean up everything at once."
"I'm sorry..."
"Whaaat? This'll save us time and effort.@k@vS10/43/410400430. ...Actually, Otobe, can I ask you a favor?"
Sonozaki-san held a hand to her mouth and whispered something to Otobe, who was hanging his head down, perhaps regretting his blunder.
"...Got it?"
"That switch over there turns on all the lights in the shrine grounds.@k@vS10/43/410400433. On my signal, flip that switch and run with all your strength."
"Run...? Which way...?"
"To the thicket over there, or anywhere really.@k@vS10/43/410400435. These people are coming here to shoot us all.@k@vS10/43/410400436. Everyone, just scatter and run away."
Otobe swallowed his spit, then looked over at me.
I nodded as hard as I could.
I truly might lose my life right here...
Despite that premonition, I felt strangely calm.
Maybe... this is actually a dream...?
Sonozaki-san's sword and Miyuki-chan's gun felt so out of place, they seemed more like toys.
Besides... why would Miyuki-chan even have a gun in the first place...?
"Now!"
Otobe pressed a switch on the side of the entrance.
There were a lot of switches by the entrance and Otobe wasn't sure which ones to press... so he started flipping them at random.
Electric lights scattered throughout the shrine grounds started lighting up, one by one...
*bang* *bang* *baaang*
Sounds like exploding firecrackers echoed from all sides.
So, this isn't a dream after all...!
How many enemies are out there...?!
"Ugh, uwaah?!"
"Hurry! Run away!"
As soon as Sonozaki-san shouted that, Otobe and I bolted out of the assembly hall.
To the right...?! To the left...?!
Which way should we go...?!
"Ryuunosuke-san, this way!@k@vS10/40/410300755. Run straight ahead!"
I turned in the direction of Miyuki-chan's voice... and ran, straight through the rain!
I heard an enemy bullet strike the ground nearby as I ran past a telephone pole.
"Uwaaah?!"
"Ryuunosuke-san!@k@vS10/40/410300757. Ghh!!"
Miyuki-chan pointed her handgun toward the darkness and fired several shots. The gun smoke wafted to the tip of my nose.
"Out of the way, it's dangerous!@k@vS10/40/410300759. Two more to the left... 1, 2, 3!!"
A few more shots rang out. *bang* *baang*
Through the smoke and rain, I could see a man in a black suit.
A bright red spurt of blood emerged from the tip of his shoulder, then he collapsed from Miyuki-chan's bullet.
Miyuki-chan skillfully swapped in a new magazine.
A man in black used that brief gap to point his gun in this direction...
"Uwah?!"
*thump*
Someone came at Miyuki-chan and me from the side and pulled us down to the ground.
A bullet fired by the man in black struck the patch of mud where I was standing just a moment ago.
"...Be careful."
"...Thanks."
...We dodged that attack by the skin of our teeth. Sonozaki-san was the one who tackled us.
Sonozaki-san charged toward the men in black with her katana in hand.
Five or six men had already fallen throughout the shrine grounds, so the number still standing... was probably around three or four.
"Most of your comrades have already fallen.@k@vS10/43/410400441. ...If the rest of you don't value your lives, come on out."
"D-damn it... Die!"
A man in black pointed his gun toward Sonozaki-san and pulled the trigger.
*click*
I heard the sound of the gun firing empty.
Sonozaki-san had a huge grin on her face as she slashed the man in black with her katana.
"Hyaa...!"
"Gaah?!"
Sonozaki-san's sword sliced through the pit of his stomach. The man's body folded over as he fell to his knees.
Cold rain and smoke bore down on the few men in black still standing.
"Damn you...!"
"...Over here!!"
Another man in black turned his muzzle toward Sonozaki-san, but Miyuki-chan fired at him without hesitation.
The next moment, right before our eyes, the handgun flew from the man's hand and danced in the air.
The two of them quickly looked at one another and lightly smiled.
...It was like watching an action movie.
Sonozaki-san and Miyuki-chan were fighting spectacularly, right before my eyes.
Even though they were fighting for their lives... every move they made was beautiful.
...Soon enough, the men in black recognized the gap in their capabilities, and they all fled at once.
Sonozaki-san flicked her thoroughly soaked sword, then wiped the blade clean with a tissue.
Miyuki-chan once again reloaded her still-smoking gun as she slowly walked toward me.
I had no choice but to watch them, dumbfounded.
"Just... who the hell... are you guys...?"
"Mion-san... Sorimachi-san..."
Otobe's voice was trembling. ...The same most likely true for me.
Sonozaki-san took a quick glance toward Miyuki-chan. But she quickly moved on and smiled toward Otobe.
"We're all right... for now."
That didn't answer any of my questions.
Who were those people that attacked us...?
And... who was Miyuki-chan, really...?
...However, I could tell the current atmosphere wasn't right for seeking answers.
Though I didn't feel like I'd be getting a chance to ask any time soon...
"F-for now... let's head back to the assembly hall."
"Let's turn off the lights around the shrine grounds."
"...Understood!"
I immediately acknowledged her request, like some yakuza henchman.
...I don't think I've responded like that since elementary school.
As soon as we returned to the assembly room, we discovered... a cup of tea spilled by our feet.
"Towada-san...?!"
And... right next to Kurosawa's corpse...... we found Towada-san laying on the ground.
